behavior_summary: This script module implements a collection of book-reading handlers. Each function prepares a specific piece of in-game text (lore, letters, or spell descriptions) and invokes the engine UI routine that displays book content to a player.

When a handler runs it sets book-related info on the target object, arranges the player context (pid), allocates/creates a runtime string pointer for the literal text, calls Book::read(book_object_ptr, string_ptr) to present the text to the player, frees the temporary string, and then propagates/handles the return value.

Handlers are data-driven: each function differs only in the literal string payload (title/body) it pushes before calling Book::read. No global game flags are read or written by these handlers in the observed excerpt.
notable_control_flow: Linear per-handler flow: set book info -> push implicit player id -> push literal text -> convert literal to pointer -> call Book::read -> free temporary string -> handle/propagate retval.
No branching or global-state based logic in these handlers; they act as thin wrappers around a single display call.
Multiple separate helper functions (U8P_SGBOOK::08A3, ::0AC9, ::0CE0, etc.) follow the same flow with different text payloads.
open_questions: Which exact event(s) trigger each function (e.g., on-use, on-read, or on-enter area)? Inference: they run when the player interacts/reads the corresponding book object, but the precise event id is not explicit in the excerpt.
Is the 'set info' opcode used for visual/UI state, object metadata, or to mark the object as currently open? The engine-level effect of the set-info step is inferred but not confirmed.
Are there any player-specific permission checks (e.g., skill or quest gating) performed outside these handlers before they are invoked?
Exact lifetime/ownership semantics of 'str to ptr' and 'free string' (allocator used and whether Book::read expects ownership transfer) are inferred but not proven from the snippet.

main_function.name: U8P_SGBOOK::0080
main_function.purpose: Present the book titled 'THE SAGA OF BONE CRUSHER' to the player by preparing its text payload and calling the engine Book::read UI routine for the target book object.
entry_conditions: Inferred: invoked when a player interacts with or attempts to read the corresponding in-world book object (i.e., a read/use interaction).
Pattern: an on-read/book-open handler called with the target book object pointer and an implicit player id (pid).

function_map.name: Book::read
function_map.role: Engine/UI routine that opens/displays a book text to the player (called with book object context and a char* string).

function_map.name: U8P_SGBOOK::str to ptr
function_map.role: Runtime helper that converts an inline literal string into an allocated pointer suitable for Book::read.

function_map.name: U8P_SGBOOK::free string
function_map.role: Runtime helper that frees temporary/allocated string memory produced by 'str to ptr'.

external_calls.purpose: Display/open the prepared book text for the specified book object and player context; primary UI call that shows the content to the player.
Allocate or otherwise convert the inline/literal pushed text to a char* pointer suitable for passing to Book::read.
Free the temporary memory allocated by 'str to ptr' after Book::read returns.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script module implements a collection of book-reading handlers. Each function prepares a specific piece of in-game text (lore, letters, or spell descriptions) and invokes the engine UI routine that displays book content to a player.
